plan.title: Keystone (sequential): port the task check orchestrator to TS

narratives.Description: The keystone: task check is dispatched by scripts/_project_context.py --dispatch-task-check, which has no TS module. Until it is ported, deleting Python breaks the primary pre-commit gate. This story ports the check orchestrator to a TS module (deft-ts check) and repoints the root Taskfile check target. It must land last because its TS replacement invokes the gate verbs that s1-s4 expose; running it earlier would call verbs that do not yet exist.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Read scripts/_project_context.py to enumerate the exact gate sequence, context-detection (framework vs vendored install), and exit semantics it implements for --dispatch-task-check. 2. Port it to packages/core/src/check/orchestrator.ts plus a `check` dispatcher verb that runs the same ordered gate list through the TS verbs, preserving the framework-source vs consumer split (#1519) and three-state exit. 3. Repoint the Taskfile.yml check target from `uv run python _project_context.py --dispatch-task-check` to `node .../bin.js check`; run the full task check and confirm green parity with the prior Python-dispatched run; add vitest coverage and a CHANGELOG entry.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer, I want task check itself to run on the TS dispatcher, so that no live gate depends on Python and the #1731 delete becomes a mechanical removal.

items.title: Given `task check` on a feature branch, when it runs, then it invokes `node .../bin.js check` (no _project_context.py --dispatch-task-check) and exercises the same ordered gate set with identical pass/fail outcomes.

narrative.Acceptance: Given `task check` on a feature branch, when it runs, then it invokes `node .../bin.js check` (no _project_context.py --dispatch-task-check) and exercises the same ordered gate set with identical pass/fail outcomes.

items.title: Given the framework-source vs vendored-consumer contexts (#1519), when task check runs in each, then it selects the correct gate set as the Python orchestrator did.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the framework-source vs vendored-consumer contexts (#1519), when task check runs in each, then it selects the correct gate set as the Python orchestrator did.

items.title: Given `grep -rE 'uv .*run python .*_project_context.py' Taskfile.yml`, when run, then it returns no live --dispatch-task-check match.

narrative.Acceptance: Given `grep -rE 'uv .*run python .*_project_context.py' Taskfile.yml`, when run, then it returns no live --dispatch-task-check match.

expected_outputs: task check fully TS-dispatched and green

references.title: Issue #1854: feat(ts-migration): Wave 8.6 -- finish the partial Python->TS live flip (precondition for #1731 delete)
